claim: Developmentalist East Asian states (South Korea, Taiwan, Singapore, China) pursuing active industrial policy — export-discipline, selective credit, state-directed FDI screening, targeted sector promotion — achieved higher long-run real per-capita GDP growth over 1960-2019 than otherwise-comparable countries starting at similar income levels in 1960. The comparable-control set is constructed from Maddison 1960 per-capita GDP neighbours plus similar initial human-capital and geographic endowment, drawn from Latin America (ARG, BRA, MEX, CHL), South Asia (IND, PAK, LKA), Southeast Asia non-developmentalist (PHL, THA in the earlier period), and Africa (GHA, EGY). The hypothesis is that the developmentalist subset's long-run growth trajectory exceeds the control group by a margin not explained by controls for initial human capital, geography, and institutional quality alone.

covers_claims_inbound.claim_text: Park Chung-hee's 1961–1979 heavy-and-chemical-industry drive in Korea produced durable industrial capability (shipbuilding, steel, petrochemicals) that generated export competitiveness by 1985, outperforming Latin American comparators on the same period.
covers_claims_inbound.claim_notes: Audit FAIL vs industrial_policy_developmentalist_states_growth (claim is Park-era Korea HCI sector-capability episode 1961-1979 while hypothesis tests aggregate long-run per-capita GDP growth across developmentalist states). Phase 4C candidate for new hypothesis: korea_hci_drive_capability_effect, or relink to infant_industry_protection_capability_effect.

covers_claims_inbound.claim_text: Taiwan's ITRI-led semiconductor strategy (1973 founding, TSMC 1987 spinoff) produced a frontier-capability industry that market-led alternatives did not generate in comparable economies.
covers_claims_inbound.claim_notes: Audit REVIEW vs industrial_policy_developmentalist_states_growth (outcome/generality gap: Taiwan semiconductor sector capability vs aggregate East Asian per-capita GDP growth). Phase 4C candidate for new hypothesis: taiwan_itri_frontier_capability_effect.

covers_claims_inbound.claim_text: Park Chung-hee developmentalist-state institutions 1961-1979 (EPB, HCI Plan, export-discipline) are the primary driver of Korean growth takeoff, not factor accumulation alone.
covers_claims_inbound.claim_notes: Audit flagged as FAIL — claim scope Korea-Park-specific 1961-1979 vs hypothesis cross-country East Asian developmentalist panel 1960-2019. Phase 4C: candidate for relink/new hypothesis/rewrite.

claim: Japanese stagnation 1990-2020 (mean GDP growth under 1%) coincided with stable or improving wellbeing indicators (life expectancy, life satisfaction), refuting the claim that zero-growth necessarily degrades human outcomes.

covers_claims_inbound.claim_text: Japanese stagnation 1990-2020 (mean GDP growth under 1%) coincided with stable or improving wellbeing indicators (life expectancy, life satisfaction), refuting the claim that zero-growth necessarily degrades human outcomes.

claim: The cumulative Venezuelan real-GDP contraction over the 2013- 2023 Maduro era (>70% peak-to-trough from the 2013 peak) is decomposed into four channels: (a) the exogenous 2014-2016 oil price shock, (b) the 2013-2019 monetary-fiscal-fusion channel culminating in the 2017-2019 Cagan-threshold hyperinflation, (c) the post-2015 US sanctions escalation (SDN 2017, PDVSA designation January 2019, secondary sanctions on tanker operators 2019-2020), and (d) post-2017 political destabilisation (2017 constitutional crisis, 2019 Guaidó parallel-government episode, 2024 election dispute). The pre-registered claim is that channels (b) and (d) — the domestic monetary-fiscal-fusion and political-destabilisation channels — together account for a larger share of the cumulative loss than channels (a) + (c) — the exogenous oil-shock and sanctions channels — combined. A falsification would indicate the collapse is more exogenously driven than the framework's institutional-policy coding implies.

covers_claims_inbound.claim_text: The 2013-2023 Maduro-era Venezuelan cumulative >70% output collapse is predominantly driven by domestic monetary-fiscal fusion and political-destabilisation channels rather than by the exogenous oil shock or US sanctions alone; the institutional channel is load-bearing.

The Maduro-era Venezuelan collapse 2013-2023 is substantially attributable to the US sanctions regime (post-2015, intensifying 2019) rather than to domestic policy content alone — economic warfare is a material share of the observed contraction, consistent with Marxian emphasis on imperial-financial power as constraint on peripheral-economy policy space.
covers_claims_inbound.claim_notes: Marxian prediction partially aligns with Rodriguez (2024) sanctions-share argument; the pre-registered decomposition allows this alternative to prevail if the data support it.

claim: Argentine quarterly real GDP, having contracted in 2024H1 under the Milei stabilisation shock, recovers along a trajectory that by Q4 2025 closes at least 50% of the peak-to-trough output gap observed during the shock's worst quarter, and by Q4 2026 returns to or exceeds the pre-Milei (Q4 2023) real-GDP level. The trajectory is faster than the LatAm peer donor pool (Brazil, Chile, Colombia, Peru, Mexico, Uruguay) weighted average for an equivalent shock absorbed without reform content. The hypothesis is consistent with the Austrian / monetarist reading that post- stabilisation recoveries are front-loaded once the fiscal- dominance distortion is removed and with empirical findings on sharp-stabilisation recovery patterns (e.g., Peru 1990-1993, Bolivia 1985-1989).

covers_claims_inbound.claim_text: Argentine real GDP under Milei shock-therapy recovers faster than the LatAm peer average after the initial 2024H1 contraction, consistent with Austrian claim that post-stabilisation recoveries are front-loaded once fiscal-dominance distortion is removed.

Argentine output recovery post-Milei shock follows the Volcker-disinflation template: transition recession followed by recovery once inflation expectations re-anchor and the new nominal anchor is credible.
covers_claims_inbound.claim_notes: Audit flagged as FAIL — claim scope names Volcker template (USA 1979) as analogy anchor while hypothesis is Argentina-only [2015, 2027] with gdp_growth outcome; claim outcome also foregrounds inflation re-anchoring. Phase 4C: candidate for rewrite to drop Volcker analogy or new hypothesis pairing Volcker and Milei as twin shock-therapy recovery cases.

covers_claims_inbound.claim_text: Milei shock therapy in Argentina from 2023 produces poverty increase and real-wage decline in its first two years, consistent with Marxist-Leninist critique of neoliberal restoration.
covers_claims_inbound.claim_notes: Audit flagged as FAIL vs current hypothesis (which tests Argentine real-GDP recovery trajectory vs LatAm peer donor pool; whereas this claim tests Milei-era poverty increase and real-wage decline). Phase 4C candidate for new hypothesis: milei_shock_therapy_poverty_and_real_wage_effect.

covers_claims_inbound.claim_text: Pinochet-era Chile's rapid liberalisation produced an initial growth collapse (1975, 1982) and only recovered after selective state re-engagement; the pure-Chicago prescription underperformed its East Asian contemporaries.
covers_claims_inbound.claim_notes: Audit FAIL vs washington_consensus_vs_developmental_state_performance (hypothesis is Menem-era Argentina, not Pinochet-era Chile). Phase 4C candidate for new hypothesis: pinochet_chile_rapid_liberalisation_growth_collapse, or relink to a broader Washington-Consensus-underperformance cross-country hypothesis.
